monitoring.title: Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: Audit trail and quick links for platform operations.
monitoring.externalMetricsNote: API error rates, database CPU, storage, and site traffic are available in your Supabase project dashboard and hosting provider. Use this page for admin audit history and external status checks.
monitoring.overviewStatDeploymentsHint: From Vercel API for the first project
monitoring.trafficChartDeploymentsDescription: Bucket counts from the current deployment list (live API).
monitoring.trafficChartSnapshotsDescription: Deployment sample size from each vercel snapshot (cron or “Record snapshot”).
monitoring.auditDescription: Actions recorded via the app for application administrators.
monitoring.platformHealthDescription: Live checks via an admin-only Edge Function (Management API, Vercel API, and app database).
monitoring.logsDescription: Filter admin audit entries and teacher activity feed without logging everything through admin audit.
monitoring.logsFiltersDescription: Toggle streams and narrow activity by type.
monitoring.logsActivityFeedDescription: Teacher and system-recorded events (including student-related triggers).
monitoring.logsActivityEmpty: No activity events in this range.
monitoring.healthDescription: Live probe plus stored DB latency history (snapshots from Edge Function).
monitoring.healthHistoryDescription: From management_api snapshots (dbLatencyMs). Run snapshots on a schedule or use the button above.
monitoring.healthNoSnapshots: No snapshots yet. Deploy collect-metric-snapshot and record a snapshot, or set up a cron job.
monitoring.trafficDescription: Live Vercel projects and recent deployments (API; not full Web Analytics).
monitoring.trafficProjectsDescription: Projects visible to your Vercel token.
monitoring.trafficDeploymentsDescription: Latest deployments for the first listed project.
monitoring.trafficChartNoData: Not enough data for a chart.

analytics.filtersDescription: Refine charts and class metrics by syllabus section, student, or assignment.
analytics.emptyModuleOrScope: No assignments in this scope. Choose another section or link assignments in the curriculum.
analytics.regenerateClassWideNote: Regenerating 5D scores always processes the whole class, not only the current filter.
analytics.coverageInScope: {{covered}} / {{enrolled}} students have scored work in the current view
analytics.compareHint: Compare class-wide average 5D between two syllabus sections. Unplaced can be included if assignments are not linked.
analytics.compareSameSection: Choose two different sections to compare.
analytics.averageScoresDesc: Average soft skills scores across all students with data
analytics.individualProfile: Individual Student Soft Skills Performance Profile
analytics.comingSoonDesc: AI-driven analysis of your class's soft skill development trends and recommendations will appear here.
analytics.regenerateScoresConfirmDescription: This runs for the whole class (not only your current filter). 5D scores are recomputed by AI from each student’s assignment chat, only for submissions that already have feedback. This replaces the stored 5D snapshot for those submissions and may take a while if there are many students.

narrative.error: Could not load AI interpretation. Default dimension descriptions are shown.
narrative.basedOnSources: Synthesized from {{count}} evaluation excerpts in this view
narrative.scoresOnlyHint: No evaluation text excerpts in this view. The interpretation uses aggregate scores and your filters only.

nuance.baselinesClassWide: The numbers in the table use your current module, student, and activity filters. Use them to compare students or focus on a single activity.
nuance.insightScope: A Nuance flag is based on that student’s recent pattern across this class, not a single table row. The same insight appears in every view for that student.
nuance.classReferenceScope: This line is for the whole class (all activities that have Nuance metrics). The table below uses only your current unit, student, and activity filters.
nuance.classReference: Class reference (all activities in this class in Nuance’s data): average response {{latency}}, time away from tab {{idle}}, work completed {{completion}} ({{n}} data points).
nuance.classReferenceCompact: Class: {{latency}} response · {{idle}} away from tab · {{completion}} done
nuance.howNuanceBody: Nuance uses signals from the assignment experience: time to reply after a coach message, time with the tab in the background, how often an activity is opened, and whether work is finished, plus optional cues when phrasing in chat suggests confusion. It is not a grade. Flags are simple checks compared to the student and class, then phrased as practical teaching ideas. When in doubt, combine Nuance with your read of submissions and in-class discussion.

metricLabels.baseline_idle_ratio: Student’s usual time away from tab
metricLabels.high_idle_sessions: Sessions with high time away from tab
metricLabels.avg_response_latency_ms: Avg. time to reply after a coach message
metricLabels.scaffolding_note: Many cues with completion on track can mean scaffolding in use, not a problem.
metricLabels.scaffolding_signal: Re-explanation requests with progress on track (normal)

nuance.title: Student Behavioral Insights
nuance.description: Nuance turns live activity patterns into short, actionable ideas for the classroom
nuance.noData: No behavioral data available yet. Data will appear as students complete activities.
nuance.emptyNoAssignmentsInFilter: No activities match the current filters, so there is nothing to show in this table.
nuance.emptyNoDataInFilter: No Nuance metrics for the activities in this filter yet. The class summary line still counts all activities in the class that have Nuance data—it is not limited to the section or student you selected.
nuance.emptyNoDataForStudentInFilter: No Nuance rows for this student in the activities above. Other students may have data in this section. Broader class numbers are unchanged. Check that this student opened these assignments (with Nuance tracking on: active submission, no feedback yet) and refresh.
nuance.emptyNoDataForAssignmentInFilter: No Nuance data for this activity in the current filter. Try a different section or “All in this section”.
nuance.emptyNoDataForPairInFilter: No Nuance row for this student and activity. Confirm they used chat on this assignment after tracking was enabled, then refresh.
nuance.notAllEnrolledInTable: Only students with at least one Nuance row for the activities in this filter are listed. Others on your roster can still have Nuance from assignments in a different unit or that are not linked to this section in the syllabus.
nuance.hintStudentNuanceInOtherScope: This student has Nuance data elsewhere in the class. Widen the unit/activity filter (or use “all assignments in class”) to see it, and confirm the assignment is placed in the right syllabus section if you expect it under this unit.
nuance.noRecommendation: No Nuance flag for this student — patterns look in range. Table numbers still reflect your current filters.

cueDetail.title: Understanding cues (detail)
cueDetail.privacy: We never store the message text. Times and turn index help you place the cue in the activity chat.
cueDetail.noneInScope: No understanding cues recorded in this filter yet.

tooltips.student: The student's name, or the activity name when filtered to a specific student.
tooltips.responseTime: Average time between the AI sending a message and the student replying.
tooltips.idleRatio: Percentage of session time the student spent inactive or tabbed away.
tooltips.completion: Percentage of started assignments the student actually finished.
tooltips.sessions: Average number of times the student opened each assignment.
tooltips.cuesInChat: How often the student’s messages suggested confusion or a need to re-explain, in this activity’s chat.
tooltips.cuesInChatColumn: Per-activity: how often the student’s phrasing looked like a clarification request. In the all-students view, the number is the average of those per-activity counts in your current filter. Expand a row to see when each cue happened; the list total is not the same as the rounded average in the cell.
tooltips.status: Typical: patterns are in range. Nuance insight: a recommendation is available — expand the row to read it.

vision.label: Vision
vision.description: Imagining new possibilities and bold ideas; creative and adaptive thinking

values.label: Values
values.description: Guided by ethics and integrity; building trust and understanding boundaries

thinking.label: Thinking
thinking.description: Strong analysis, deep insight, and sound judgment; critical and analytical skills

connection.label: Connection
connection.description: Empathy, clear communication, and effective collaboration

action.label: Action
action.description: Turning plans into results with focus, determination, and practical skills

cra.title: Content-Related Abilities (CRA)
cra.description: Assessment of hard skills based on student performance
cra.unplacedSection: Not linked to a syllabus section
cra.scopeSummary: {{skills}} CRA skills across {{assignments}} assignments, {{students}} students. Strongest domain on average: {{topDomain}}. Lowest: {{bottomDomain}}.

table.scrollHint: Scroll horizontally to see all columns if the table is wider than the screen.

studentOnboarding.title: Student Profile Setup
studentOnboarding.stepOf: Step {{current}} of {{total}}: Let's learn about your learning preferences

step1.learningMethodsQuestion: Which types of activities or methods help you learn best?
step1.visualDesc: I learn best by reading textbooks, looking at diagrams, graphs, and pictures
step1.auditoryDesc: I learn best by listening to explanations and discussions
step1.kinestheticDesc: I learn best by doing hands-on practice and activities
step1.videoDesc: I learn best by watching videos and demonstrations

step2.soloVsGroupQuestion: Do you learn better when studying alone, or do you prefer learning with others?
step2.soloDesc: I prefer working on material by myself
step2.groupDesc: I like group activities and discussions that help me understand
step2.bothDesc: I like a mix of independent and group learning
step2.scheduledVsFlexibleQuestion: Do you like following a set schedule or plan, or do you prefer to be flexible?
step2.scheduledDesc: I thrive with a routine and a set plan
step2.flexibleDesc: I prefer to decide what to study as I go

step3.motivationQuestion: What motivates you to put in your best effort when learning something new?
step3.curiosityDesc: I love learning about new topics that interest me
step3.gradesDesc: I want to get high grades and academic success
step3.encouragementDesc: I am motivated by praise and encouragement from others
step3.personalGoalsDesc: I am driven by my own goals and aspirations
step3.competitionDesc: I am motivated by friendly competition with peers

step4.helpQuestion: If you're struggling to understand a concept or skill, what is the best way someone can help you?
step4.hints: Give me hints or clues to figure it out myself
step4.differentWay: Explain it in a different way or with different examples
step4.moreTime: Let me figure it out myself with more time
step4.teacherQuestion: What do you look for in a teacher or coach?
step4.patient: Someone who is patient and understanding
step4.challenging: Someone who pushes me to achieve my best
step4.clear: Someone who explains everything very clearly
step4.fun: Someone who makes learning fun and engaging

step5.feedbackQuestion: How do you prefer to receive feedback or corrections on your work?
step5.discussion: A short conversation where the teacher goes over how I did
step5.goalQuestion: What is one goal you hope to achieve through this learning experience?
step5.goalPlaceholder: e.g., Improve my grade, master a skill, gain confidence, prepare for college...

step6.specialNeedsQuestion: Do you have any specific needs or preferences that help you learn better?
step6.specialNeedsPlaceholder: e.g., Short breaks, visual aids, hands-on practice, quiet environment...
step6.additionalNotesQuestion: Is there anything else you'd like the teacher or AI to know about how you like to learn or study?
step6.additionalNotesPlaceholder: Share any other notes, learning difficulties, anxieties, or preferences...

step1.studentLevelPlaceholder: e.g., Middle School, High School, University

step2.teachingGoalsQuestion: What are your main teaching goals?

aboutUs.heroSubtitle: We are a team of educators, technologists, and dreamers dedicated to reshaping the future of learning. We believe that every student deserves a personalized education tailored to their unique needs and potential.
aboutUs.missionText: To democratize access to high-quality, personalized education by leveraging the power of AI to support teachers and empower students.
aboutUs.visionText: A world where every learner has a dedicated AI companion that guides their growth, not just academically, but holistically—fostering critical thinking, empathy, and resilience.

story.paragraph1: Perleap was born from a simple observation: while technology has transformed nearly every aspect of our lives, the fundamental model of the classroom has remained largely unchanged for a century.
story.paragraph2: Our founders, a mix of former teachers and AI researchers, saw that educators were overwhelmed with administrative tasks and struggling to provide individual attention to every student in a crowded classroom.
story.paragraph3: We asked ourselves: 'What if we could give every student a personal tutor and every teacher a superpower assistant?'
story.paragraph4: Today, Perleap is used by forward-thinking schools around the world to create engaging, effective, and human-centric learning environments.

studentCentric.title: Student-Centric
studentCentric.description: Every decision we make starts with the question: 'How does this help the learner?'

empathy.title: Empathy First
empathy.description: We believe that emotional intelligence is just as important as academic intelligence.

innovation.title: Bold Innovation
innovation.description: We are not afraid to challenge the status quo to build a better future for education.

excellence.title: Pursuit of Excellence
excellence.description: We hold ourselves to the highest standards because our students deserve nothing less.

cta.title: Join Us on Our Journey
cta.subtitle: Whether you're a school leader, a teacher, or a parent, we'd love to show you how Perleap can make a difference.
